project.name: Campus Study Space Finder Group Project
project.output: A clickable prototype and short presentation that help students find suitable campus study spaces while explaining data limits.
project.audience: A four-person course project team preparing a class demo
project.collaborationMode: Local-first GitHub JSON sync through OpenCollab

brief.assignment: Design and present a Campus Study Space Finder for students who need a quiet, available, and accessible place to work.
constraints: no live sensor data; availability must be shown as estimated or unknown
prototype and report must use the same data contract
presentation claims must match evaluation evidence

workPackages.label: Research and scope
workPackages.intent: Clarify users, success criteria, and interview plan.

workPackages.label: Data and assumptions
workPackages.intent: Create the study-space dataset, schema, and availability-confidence rules.

workPackages.label: Prototype
workPackages.intent: Build a searchable map/list prototype and handoff flow.

workPackages.label: Evaluation and presentation
workPackages.intent: Test the prototype, write the story, and prepare the final presentation.

classificationHints: Use dependency edges when one artifact is a prerequisite for another.
Use boundary edges when two owners must align wording, schema, claims, or evaluation criteria.
Use sync edges when two active tasks need a short meeting before either task can safely continue.
Do not assign the same file, claim, or contract decision to two owners without making the boundary explicit.
